授業目的: This course is designed to familiarize students with international relations theory, international relations history leading up to the America-led international order, and how the Trump era is transforming international relations. Note: This is a very challenging course with a **heavy reading and writing workload.**
到達目標: Students will learn about international relations theory, history, and U.S. politics, while also developing the analytical skills needed to understand and evaluate the changes taking place in the Trump era.
関連科目: Global Politics, International Relations, Conflict and Peace Studies, International Security, Modern History, International Organizations in World Politics.
授業の概要・背景:
This course focuses on international relations theory, the forces that have shaped the development of the international order throughout various periods in history, U.S. domestic politics, and the changes taking place in international relations in the Trump era.

Note: This is a very challenging course with a **heavy reading and writing workload.**
授業方法: IMPORTANT: This is a lecture-based course, primarily in face-to-face format, though some lectures may be in on-demand (online) format -- the schedule for which lectures are in person and which are online is subject to change, so please make sure to regularly check this course's LUNA page for announcements about any changes to the course schedule.

教科書/Required texts0:
Francois de Soete, “The Limits of Interpretation and Thucydides: An Analysis of Realist Themes in the History of the Peloponnesian War,” The Journal of International Studies 13 (2024): 33-47. Available online at: https://kwansei.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/2000246
Francois de Soete, “After America : Domestic Instability in the Trump Era and the Future of U.S. Leadership in the Indo-Pacific,” The Journal of International Studies 14 (2022), pp. 95-114. [URL will be provided on LUNA]
Francois de Soete, “Trump, Biden, and U.S. Foreign Policy: Analyzing the Role of the Modern Presidency on America's Standing in the World,” The Journal of International Studies 11 (2022), pp. 1-11. Available online at: https://kwansei.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/30079
Francois de Soete, “The Trump Presidency, the Coronavirus, and the New America: Can the U.S. Military Still Preserve the Status Quo in East Asia,” The Journal of International Studies 10 (2021), pp. 37-52. Available online at: https://kwansei.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/29325
Students can access the following textbook from the SIS library (it is NOT necessary for students to purchase the following textbook):
John Baylis, Steve Smith, and Patricia Owens, “The Globalization of World Politics, Eighth Edition” (Oxford University Press, 2020). [Available at the SIS Library]
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Global Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
